user_input: Wat are teh rules for travling with pets on American Eagle?
reference_contexts:
WEB REFERENCE Reservations
*General Information
Policy and procedures for traveling with carry-on/cabin pets on domestic or international flights operated by
American Airlines/American Eagle.
 
 
Related Information
Related Topics Information and Links
Checked Pets (Checked Pets as Baggage) CHECKED PET
Customer Check-in Customer Check-in
Celebrity Animals CELEBRITY PET
Military/Police, Search/Rescue and Drug/Explosive
Detection Animals SPECIALTY ANIMALS
Service Animals and Puppies in Training for Service
Animals SPCL ASSIST ANIMALS
 
Acceptance Criteria
Acceptance Critieria:
American Airlines ONLY accepts cats and dogs as cabin pets
No other animal species is accepted as a cabin pet
Cabin pets must be well-mannered and free of odor, parasites and illnesses
Animals displaying any type of disruptive behavior will be refused transportation as a cabin pet.
Disruptive behavior includes, but is not limited to:
Barking/meowing constantly and/or loudly
Biting or attempting to bite
Growling/hissing
Scratching at or jumping on customers or employees
CABIN PET
10/29/24, 1:19 AM WebReference Release WR2408.0.A: Reservations
https://webref.aa.com/res/resui/index.jsp 1/13
response:
Pet Travel Policy Summary
American Eagle Pet Requirements

American Eagle has established specific guidelines for pet travel that passengers must follow:

Carrier Requirements: Pets must be transported in airline-approved carriers
Size Limitations: Specific size restrictions apply to pet carriers
Additional Costs: Pet travel fees are required
Booking Process: Advance reservations must be made for pet travel

These policies ensure safe and regulated pet transportation while maintaining compliance with airline standards.
reference: American Eagle, operated by American Airlines, only accepts cats and dogs as cabin pets. These pets must be well-mannered, free of odor, parasites, and illnesses. Animals displaying disruptive behavior, such as constant barking/meowing, biting, growling, hissing, or scratching, will be refused transportation as cabin pets.

user_input: What CDC say about dogs from high-risk rabies countries?
reference_contexts:
Relieving themselves outside of designated animal relief area
Customers, both revenue and non-revenue, traveling with cabin pets are required
To check-in with an agent at the ticket counter
Complete the animal inspection process
Properly documented for travel to its final destination
Cabin pet MUST remain inside the closed/zipped kennel at all times, including:
Gate lounge area
During boarding and deplaning of the aircraft
All Admirals Clubs and Flagship Lounges 
Onboard the aircraft
There is no age requirement for passengers traveling with cabin pets
Travelers of any age, as long as they are in a seat (no infant in arms or lap child), can travel with a
cabin pet
Note:  Pet strollers are NOT PERMITTED for cabin pets. The pet stroller is consider checked baggage and all
applicable checked baggage fees apply
CDC High Risk Countries
U.S. Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC) strives to protect families and pets by preventing the
reintroduction of dog rabies into the United States. 
American Airlines is not accepting Cabin Pet/Carry-on dogs from high-risk rabies countries, or visited high-
risk rabies countries in the last 6 months.  If the customer is traveling from any of the countries listed below
with a dog, advise unable to accept cabin pet. Exceptions are for service animals and for U.S. Military or State
Department Foreign Service Personnel on official orders traveling with cabin pet. Must comply with CDC
requirements. If you have a customer who says traveling with a cabin pet and is on U.S. Military or State
Department Foreign Service Personnel on official orders, see Reservation Action for handling. 
Â The CDC rabies restricted countries for AA destinations.
Belize Cuba Dominican Republic Ecuador
El Salvador Guatemala Guyana Haiti
Honduras Peru Suriname  
Note: Customers traveling with dog(s) from a high-risk rabies country or visited a high-risk rabies country in
the last 6 months not listed in the AA destinations will not be accepted to enter the U.S. 
Note: Cats are accepted as Cabin Pet as they are not part of the high-risk for rabies. Reservation Action is in
place for those traveling with cats.  AACoRN cannot distinguish if it's a cat or dog when booking a cabin pet,
the booking for a cat as a cabin pet must be done by SAC. 
Reservation Action
Advise the customer the Cabin Pet for cats will be booked by a separate desk
Ask the customer if the Cabin Pet is a cat, if so proceed with the next step
Blind transfer to SAC during SAC hours
Except for MCLA to SAC Spanish and EUPA SAC - Warm Transfer 
SAC after hours
Document PNR historical remarks CABIN PET EXCEPTION
10/29/24, 1:19 AM WebReference Release WR2408.0.A: Reservations
https://webref.aa.com/res/resui/index.jsp 2/13
response:
Summary
CDC Dog Import Restrictions

The Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC) has implemented restrictions on transporting dogs from countries with high rabies risk. This measure is designed as a preventive strategy to avoid the reintroduction of rabies into the United States, protecting public health by controlling potential disease vectors at the border.
reference: The U.S. Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC) strives to protect families and pets by preventing the reintroduction of dog rabies into the United States. American Airlines does not accept cabin pet/carry-on dogs from high-risk rabies countries or dogs that have visited high-risk rabies countries in the last 6 months. Exceptions are made for service animals and for U.S. Military or State Department Foreign Service Personnel on official orders, provided they comply with CDC requirements.

user_input: What United States rules for dogs comin' from screwworm places?
reference_contexts:
Verify the phone contact for the customer
Queue place the PNR to DFW105/11
Advise the customer an AA Representative will be contacting them to assist with the arrangements
If the customer has a dog, advise dogs are not accepted until further notice
 
Note:  Always check the Destination Restrictions section to verify those destinations where AA policy does not
provide a PET IN CABIN option. 
 
Â 
CDC Screwworm
The U.S. Department of Agriculture Animal and Plant Health Inspection Service Veterinary Services (USDA
APHIS VS) regulates dogs from regions affected with screwworm. As per USDA APHIS VS, dogs traveling
from countries or regions where screwworm is known to exist may enter the United States if they are
accompanied by a certificate signed by a full-time salaried veterinary official of the region of origin stating:
If the dog is traveling from a High-Risk Rabies country see list below, the dog cannot travel
The dog has been inspected for screwworm within five (5) days prior to shipment to the United States
The dog is either free from screwworm 
OR
The dog was infested with screwworm, held in quarantine, and treated until free from screwworm prior to
leaving the region

SAC after hours
Document PNR historical remarks CABIN PET EXCEPTION
Verify the phone contact for the customer
Queue place the PNR to DFW105/11
Advise the customer an AA Representative will be contacting them to assist with the arrangements
 
ScrewwormÂ restricted countries for AA destinations; allows Cabin Pet travel.
Aruba Costa Rica Cuba Dominican
Republic Ecuador Guyana
Haiti Nicaragua Panama Peru Trinidad and

Age Restrictions
Pets are required to be a minimum of eight (8) weeks old for American Airlines:
Domestic travel eight (8) weeks old

10/29/24, 1:19 AM WebReference Release WR2408.0.A: Reservations
https://webref.aa.com/res/resui/index.jsp 3/13
response:
Summary: Dog Import Requirements from Screwworm-Affected Regions

Dogs originating from regions affected by screwworm must undergo mandatory inspection and obtain USDA APHIS VS (Animal and Plant Health Inspection Service Veterinary Services) certification before being permitted entry into the United States.
reference: The U.S. Department of Agriculture Animal and Plant Health Inspection Service Veterinary Services (USDA APHIS VS) regulates dogs from regions affected with screwworm. Dogs traveling from countries or regions where screwworm is known to exist may enter the United States if they are accompanied by a certificate signed by a full-time salaried veterinary official of the region of origin stating: the dog has been inspected for screwworm within five (5) days prior to shipment to the United States and is either free from screwworm or was infested with screwworm, held in quarantine, and treated until free from screwworm prior to leaving the region.

user_input: Wht r teh reqiremnts for pets enterring teh United States?
reference_contexts:

transportation

International itineraries

travel requirements in Web Timatic for each
country (including entry into the United States)

Entry requirements for dogs entry the U.S.
depends on the origin country's rabies

Risk Level assigned by the CDC 
Periodically the CDC updates a country's
assigned risk level, advise customer to
continue checking the website prior to their
departure for possible changes
Entry into Hawaii
Per AA Policy cabin pets are NEVER
PERMITTED on flights to/from any Hawaiian
destination
See Destination Restrictions for full details
Â 
Health Requirements
Health requirements for cabin pets:
Animals are visually examined at the airport during the check-in process
Animals must be free of odor, parasites and illness
Sedation - as recommended by the American Veterinary Medical Association (AVMA), AA does not accept
pets that have been sedated or tranquilized. Pets that have been given sedatives or tranquilizers are at a
higher risk of respiratory and cardiovascular problems at high altitudes
Domestic travel - A health certificate is not required for cabin pets however;
Pets must, have the proper health documentation for final destination if required
Customers should contact their veterinarian, state or country animal health commissions for
destination requirements
International travel - See Web Timatic for health certificate requirements and additional information
 
Note: A Pet Passport is not accepted in lieu of American's required documents. 
Destination Restrictions
Provided all requirements are met, AA only accepts cabin pets for travel to/from the following:
48 contiguous United States
10/29/24, 1:19 AM WebReference Release WR2408.0.A: Reservations
https://webref.aa.com/res/resui/index.jsp 4/13
response:
Pet Import Requirements Summary
Regulatory Compliance

Pets entering the United States must comply with regulations from two key federal agencies:

CDC (Centers for Disease Control and Prevention) requirements
USDA (United States Department of Agriculture) requirements
Essential Documentation

The primary requirements for pet entry include:

Vaccination records - ensuring pets are up-to-date on required immunizations
Health certification - official veterinary documentation confirming the pet's health status

This regulatory framework ensures the protection of both animal and public health when pets cross international borders into the United States.
reference: Entry requirements for dogs entering the United States depend on the origin country's rabies risk level, which is assigned by the CDC. Representatives are required to verify pet travel requirements in Web Timatic for each country, including entry into the United States. Customers should be advised to continue checking the CDC website prior to their departure for possible changes in the assigned risk level.

10/29/24, 1:19 AM WebReference Release WR2408.0.A: Reservations
https://webref.aa.com/res/resui/index.jsp 12/13
response:
Pet Travel Policy Summary
Cabin Pet Restrictions

American Airlines has specific guidelines for passengers traveling with pets in the cabin:

Allowed Animals: Only cats and dogs are permitted as cabin pets

Requirements: All cabin pets must meet:

Health requirements
Seating restrictions
Destination-specific regulations

Booking Process: Passengers can conveniently add their pets to existing reservations through American Airlines' online booking system

This policy ensures safe and regulated pet travel while providing an accessible booking method for pet owners.
reference: Travel restrictions for cabin pets on American Airlines flights include the prohibition of cabin pets on all Transatlantic, Transpacific, and Hawaii routes, as well as flights to Jamaica (though pets are permitted on flights from Jamaica). Additionally, cabin pets are not allowed to or from certain South American countries, including Argentina, Brazil, Chile, Colombia, and Uruguay. Seating restrictions also apply, such as bulkhead rows and exit rows on all aircraft, and premium cabins with lie-flat seating due to lack of under-seat storage space. Passengers can add a cabin pet to their reservation online at aa.com up to 3 hours prior to departure. Only one passenger at a time can add a pet, and the process must be repeated for additional passengers. Once added, the system displays kennel size requirements and service fee details, which must be paid at the airport. Note that reservations containing a PETC SSR cannot be changed or checked in online.

10/29/24, 1:19 AM WebReference Release WR2408.0.A: Reservations
https://webref.aa.com/res/resui/index.jsp 8/13
response:
Pet Travel Summary
International Travel Requirements

International pet travel requires adherence to destination-specific regulations, proper health certificates, and compliance with individual airline policies.

Kennel Specifications

Mainline aircraft enforce specific maximum kennel dimensions as detailed in the official Kennel Size Chart to ensure safe transport.
reference: For international itineraries, pet travel requirements include verifying pet travel requirements in Web Timatic for each country, ensuring compliance with entry requirements such as rabies risk levels assigned by the CDC, and checking for updates on the CDC website prior to departure. Pets must be visually examined at the airport during check-in, free of odor, parasites, and illness, and cannot be sedated or tranquilized. Health certificates may be required depending on the destination, and a Pet Passport is not accepted in lieu of required documents. Regarding kennel size restrictions for mainline aircraft, the dimensions are 19 inches (48.26 cm) in length, 13 inches (33.02 cm) in width, and 9 inches (22.86 cm) in height. Pets must fit comfortably inside their kennels, allowing them to stand, sit, and lie naturally without touching or protruding through the sides or top of the kennel.

user_input: What are the kennel area requirements for cabin pets, and how do travel restrictions impact pet travel to specific destinations?
reference_contexts:
<1-hop>

Kennel Area Requirements
 General
Be secure and fit under the seat on each aircraft type in the
customer's itinerary
Remain closed/zipped, with the animal inside the kennel,
and under the seat in front at all times during flight and
when requested by flight crew
Customer's with pet kennels are NEVER PERMITTED to sit in
bulkhead or exit row seating
 Space
REGULATORY: USDA Live Animal Regulations require the
pet must fit comfortably inside their kennels
Airport agents will verify space requirements
If the pet and/or kennel do not meet these requirements,
the pet will not be accepted for travel as a cabin pet
 Carry-on
Cabin pet kennel can carry-on a personal item or a carry-on
bag
Example: Cabin pet kennel + personal item or Cabin
pet kennel + carry-on bag 
 
Ventilation Kennel must have ventilation openings on three (3) or more
sides
 Material
 Hard-sided Kennels:
Must be constructed of wood, metal, rigid plastic or
composite material of similar strength
Door/opening must fasten/close securely and have easily
accessible openings for emergency removal, if necessary
Must be leak-proof and escape-proof 
 Soft-Sided Kennels:
Maximum: 18in length x 11in width x 11in height
Must be constructed of water-repellent, padded nylon with
mesh ventilation
Must contain absorbent material, such as a blanket or
towel, to prevent leakage
Weight Only restriction is on A321T in First and Business combine
weight of pet and kennel must be 20 lbs or less
Size See Kennel Size
 
Kennel Size
The maximum size of cabin pet kennel/carrier allowed depends on the operating aircraft in the customer's
itinerary. The chart below provides the maximum kennel size based on aircraft fleet:
Maximum Allowed Pet Kennel/Carrier Size

10/29/24, 1:19 AM WebReference Release WR2408.0.A: Reservations
https://webref.aa.com/res/resui/index.jsp 5/13
response:
Pet Travel Requirements Summary
Kennel Standards for Cabin Pets

Pet kennels for cabin travel must meet strict regulatory requirements including:

Specific dimensional standards to ensure adequate space
Approved materials for construction
Proper ventilation systems for pet safety
Security features to prevent escape
USDA compliance with all applicable regulations
Travel Restrictions

Pet travel may be subject to limitations including:

Destination-specific prohibitions based on local policies
Health-related restrictions due to safety concerns
Regional travel bans that may affect certain routes

These requirements ensure both pet safety and regulatory compliance during air travel.
reference: The kennel area requirements for cabin pets include being secure and fitting under the seat on each aircraft type in the customer's itinerary. The kennel must remain closed/zipped with the animal inside at all times during the flight and when requested by the flight crew. Customers with pet kennels are not permitted to sit in bulkhead or exit row seating. The kennel must meet USDA Live Animal Regulations, ensuring the pet fits comfortably inside. Hard-sided kennels must be constructed of durable materials like wood, metal, or rigid plastic, while soft-sided kennels must be made of water-repellent, padded nylon with mesh ventilation and contain absorbent material to prevent leakage. Ventilation openings are required on three or more sides, and the maximum size of the kennel depends on the operating aircraft in the customer's itinerary. Regarding travel restrictions, cabin pets are not permitted on transatlantic, transpacific, or Hawaii flights. Additionally, cabin pets are not allowed on flights to Jamaica, though they are permitted on flights from Jamaica. In South America, cabin pets are not permitted to or from cities in Argentina, Brazil, Chile, Colombia, and Uruguay. Seating restrictions also apply, as cabin pets are not allowed in bulkhead or exit rows due to lack of under-seat storage space and FAA safety requirements.

response:
Pet Travel Summary
Cabin Pet Booking Requirements

Traveling with cats in aircraft cabins requires careful attention to destination-specific restrictions and individual airline policies. Key requirements include:

Advance reservations - Most airlines require prior booking for cabin pets
Health documentation - Veterinary certificates and vaccination records typically needed
Destination compliance - Entry requirements vary by location and may include quarantine or additional permits

Pet owners should verify both airline and destination requirements well in advance of travel to ensure proper documentation and booking procedures are completed.
reference: To book a cabin pet, specifically a cat, travelers must ensure the pet is properly documented for travel to its final destination and complete the animal inspection process at the ticket counter. Cats are accepted as cabin pets since they are not considered high-risk for rabies. The booking for a cat as a cabin pet must be done by a separate desk (SAC), and travelers are advised to contact SAC during its operating hours. If SAC is unavailable, historical remarks must be documented in the PNR as 'CABIN PET EXCEPTION.' Destination restrictions impact this process as American Airlines does not accept dogs from high-risk rabies countries or regions affected by screwworm unless specific USDA APHIS VS requirements are met. Cats, however, are not subject to these restrictions, allowing them to travel as cabin pets without additional constraints related to rabies or screwworm.

response:
Pet Travel Restrictions and Procedures for Puerto Rico

Key Requirements:

Pets must comply with cabin pet policies
Reservations required through Pet PNR system
Applicable fees must be paid

This summary outlines the essential procedures for travelers bringing pets to Puerto Rico, emphasizing policy compliance, proper booking procedures, and fee payment requirements.
reference: Traveling with a pet to Puerto Rico involves specific restrictions and procedures. Cabin pets are permitted for travel within Puerto Rico, but seating restrictions apply, such as pets not being allowed in bulkhead rows or exit rows due to lack of under-seat storage space and FAA safety requirements. Additionally, fees apply for pet travel: $150 USD per kennel for tickets issued on or after February 20, 2024, and $125 USD for tickets issued before that date. If a connection exceeds four hours for domestic travel or 24 hours for international travel, the charge is applied per segment. A pet PNR must be created for cabin pets, which includes the last name 'PET' and the first name formatted as 'CBBG PASSENGERS LAST NAME' (e.g., PET/CBBG JONES). The system will add an OSI to the original passenger PNR to cross-reference with the pet PNR.

response:
Resumen de Restricciones para Mascotas en Ecuador
ClasificaciÃ³n de Riesgo de Rabia

Ecuador estÃ¡ clasificado como un paÃ­s de alto riesgo de rabia, lo que impone restricciones especÃ­ficas para el transporte de mascotas.

Restricciones de Transporte
Los perros no pueden viajar como mascotas de cabina en vuelos de American Airlines hacia Ecuador
Se aplican requisitos adicionales de salud para todas las mascotas
Existen requisitos especÃ­ficos del destino que deben cumplirse antes del viaje
Consideraciones Importantes

Los propietarios de mascotas deben verificar todos los requisitos sanitarios y de documentaciÃ³n antes de planificar un viaje a Ecuador con sus animales de compaÃ±Ã­a.
reference: Traveling with a cabin pet from Ecuador to the United States on American Airlines involves several restrictions and requirements. Firstly, Ecuador is listed as a screwworm-restricted country, which allows cabin pet travel, but dogs traveling from Ecuador must comply with USDA APHIS regulations. These regulations require a certificate signed by a full-time salaried veterinary official of Ecuador stating that the dog has been inspected for screwworm within five days prior to shipment and is either free from screwworm or has been treated and held in quarantine until free from screwworm. Additionally, Ecuador is classified as a high-risk rabies country by the CDC, and American Airlines does not accept cabin pet/carry-on dogs from high-risk rabies countries unless they are service animals or belong to U.S. Military or State Department Foreign Service Personnel on official orders. Cats, however, are accepted as cabin pets since they are not considered high-risk for rabies. Cabin pets must remain inside a closed/zipped kennel at all times during travel, including in the gate lounge area, onboard the aircraft, and in Admirals Clubs and Flagship Lounges. Pet strollers are not permitted for cabin pets and are considered checked baggage, with applicable fees applied.

reference:
Under the WebReference Release WR2408.0.A, pet kennels for cabin transportation must meet several requirements: 
1. **Size**: The maximum size of the kennel depends on the operating aircraft in the customer's itinerary, with specific dimensions provided for each fleet. For soft-sided kennels, the maximum dimensions are 18 inches in length, 11 inches in width, and 11 inches in height.
2. **Material**: Hard-sided kennels must be constructed of wood, metal, rigid plastic, or composite material of similar strength, be leak-proof and escape-proof, and have secure fastenings for emergency removal. Soft-sided kennels must be made of water-repellent, padded nylon with mesh ventilation and contain absorbent material, such as a blanket or towel, to prevent leakage.
3. **Ventilation**: Kennels must have ventilation openings on three or more sides to ensure proper airflow.
Additionally, the combined weight of the pet and kennel must not exceed 20 lbs for A321T aircraft in First and Business class. These requirements ensure compliance with USDA Live Animal Regulations and airline policies for safe and secure pet travel.

American Airlines ONLY accepts cats and dogs as cabin pets

Disruptive behavior includes, but is not limited to:
Barking/meowing constantly and/or loudly
Biting or attempting to bite
Growling/hissing
Scratching at or jumping on customers or employees

Health Requirements
Health requirements for cabin pets:
Animals are visually examined at the airport during the check-in process
Animals must be free of odor, parasites and illness
Sedation - as recommended by the American Veterinary Medical Association (AVMA), AA does not accept
pets that have been sedated or tranquilized. Pets that have been given sedatives or tranquilizers are at a
higher risk of respiratory and cardiovascular problems at high altitudes
Domestic travel - A health certificate is not required for cabin pets however;
Pets must, have the proper health documentation for final destination if required
Customers should contact their veterinarian, state or country animal health commissions for
destination requirements
International travel - See Web Timatic for health certificate requirements and additional information
 
Note: A Pet Passport is not accepted in lieu of American's required documents.

48 contiguous United States

response:
Pet Travel Requirements Summary
Age and Health Requirements

Pets must be at least 8 weeks old and in healthy condition to be eligible for travel on American Eagle flights.
reference: American Eagle requires that cats and dogs traveling as cabin pets must be at least eight weeks old, as per IATA Live Animal Regulations. There is no maximum age limit for travel. Health requirements include that pets must be free of odor, parasites, and illness, and they are visually examined at the airport during check-in. Sedated or tranquilized pets are not accepted due to the higher risk of respiratory and cardiovascular problems at high altitudes.
